item.title: U.S. trade deficit totaled $901 billion in 2025 despite Trump's tariffs
item.description: The U.S. trade deficit swelled in December, closing out a year in which the imbalance was essentially unchanged.

item.title: Hassett says authors of New York Fed tariff study should be disciplined: 'Worst paper I've ever seen'
item.description: The NEC director ripped the report, saying that central bank researchers ignored key aspects of how the duties worked

item.title: The 'boomcession': Why Americans feel left behind by a growing economy
item.description: A combination of the words "boom" and "recession" can help explain why Americans feel so sour about a growing economy.

item.title: UK inflation cools markedly in January, boosting odds of Bank of England rate cut
item.description: The U.K. inflation rate cooled to 3% in January, according to the latest figures from the Office for National Statistics (ONS).

item.title: Consumer prices rose 2.4% annually in January, less than expected
item.description: The consumer price index was expected to increase 2.5% from a year ago in January, according to the Dow Jones consensus.
